creator: yasu@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Yasuhiro Kimura from comment #6)

Before starting `git bisect` I updated host system from main-n246034-e6ab1e365c to main-n249811-a36230f75e0 and checked if emacs binary built on the former result in error if it is executed on the latter. Then emacs starts without error.

Next I rebuilt all packages on main-n249811-a36230f75e0 and reinstalled them. Naturally error happened when I started Emacs.

And finaly I reverted host system to main-n246034-e6ab1e365c and checked what happens if I executed emacs binary build on main-n249811-a36230f75e0. Then following message is displayed in *Warnings* buffer.

----------------------------------------------------------------------
Warning (initialization): An error occurred while loading `/home/yasu/.emacs':

File error: Getting attributes, Too many links, /home/yasu/.emacs
----------------------------------------------------------------------

So it seems the source of the problem exists in not kernel but userland.
